title: salvia protracta
intro: Salvia protracta, also known as blue sage, is a perennial shrubby plant that belongs to the mint family, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Salvia protracta include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. Salvia protracta can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and benefits.
